summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering a wide range of topics including a presentation on the Clermont County Senior Services Levy renewal, adoption of a resolution to create a new M-3 Limited High Impact Industrial zoning district (primarily targeting data centers), a discussion on Duke Energy's proposed electric distribution rate increase filed with PUCO, and a comprehensive presentation on the township's updated future land use plan. The board also received updates on the ongoing construction of Fire Station 18, the Quint fire apparatus build, cemetery and signage projects, and community events.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
Motion to approve payment of bills for March 10-24, 2026 passed unanimously
Resolution R-0122026 adopted unanimously to initiate zoning text amendments establishing the M-3 Limited High Impact Industrial District
action_items: John Langenhorn to continue refining the M-3 zoning district resolution as a living document
Admin staff to formulate a letter opposing Duke Energy's proposed rate increase for submission to PUCO and Ohio Township Association
Trustee Hauserman to coordinate with Clermont County Township Association and Alan Freeman at Pierce Township to organize a collective response to Duke Energy rate increase
All officials and residents encouraged to submit public comments to PUCO regarding Duke Energy rate case (26-0132-EL-AIR)
Drew (county planner) to meet with Goshen staff around April 4th to address comments on the future land use plan
Drew to obtain updated demographic data from school district consultant and incorporate into the land use plan
Final draft of future land use plan to be ready by April 7th with trustee presentation on April 14th
Chad to coordinate installation of new sign poles once delivered to complete the sign grant
Melinda/admin team to continue getting written clarification from Duke Energy (Samantha) on exact rate impact details
key_amounts: $26/year cost per $100,000 home value for Senior Services Levy renewal
$78/year cost for average $300,000 Clermont County home for Senior Services Levy
90+ cents of every Senior Services levy dollar goes back into community services
Senior Services served over 4,000 individuals, provided 40,000+ rides and nearly 70,000 meals last year
703 Adult Protective Services referrals last year
Duke Energy proposes residential customer charge increase from $8 to $14/month (75% increase on that component)
Duke Energy claims overall residential bill increase will be 3-5%
$2,000 per acre cap ($500,000 max per farm) for Clean Ohio Agricultural Easement Purchase Program
Home prices in north Clermont submarket rose 51% from 2019-2024
Over 3,800 new housing units reviewed countywide in past 12 months
Smith Bay and Charles Snyder repaving OPWC award of nearly $500,000
Linton Road bridge replacement OPWC award of nearly $600,000
$2.5 million awarded for Combs Dump Site remediation
ODOT Safer Routes to School grants reimburse up to 100% of eligible costs with $1 million project limit
M-3 district requires minimum 100-acre lot size with 300-foot setbacks and 1,000-foot separation from residential
notable_topics: Clermont County Senior Services Levy renewal presentation (May 5th ballot)
Creation of M-3 Limited High Impact Industrial Zoning District targeting data centers and high-impact industrial uses
Duke Energy proposed electric distribution rate increase filed with PUCO
Comprehensive future land use plan update presentation by county planner
Fire Station 18 construction nearing completion with May 8th grand opening planned
Quint fire apparatus build starting with late June/early July delivery expected
Medic 218 repairs underway at Ford following October accident
Main Street building wall collapse and safety meeting with county and owner
Goshen Township Historical Society fundraising events including flag donations and April 18th horse race fundraiser
Community survey results emphasizing rural character preservation and managed growth
State Route 28 corridor development and traffic concerns (34,000+ daily trips at Buckwheat Road intersection)
Cluster subdivision concept and agricultural preservation programs discussed
Infrastructure needs including sewer capacity limitations near Deerfield Road
attendees: Emily Shack (Supportive Services Director, Clermont Senior Services)
Jim Poe (Goshen Township Historical Society, 6917 Shiloh Road)
Drew (Clermont County Planner, 101 East Main Street, Batavia)
notes: The transcript was truncated before the end of the meeting. Drew the county planner was moved up on the agenda (Item J before Items F-I) to accommodate his law school class at NKU. The M-3 zoning district resolution numbers were adjusted from the original proposal during discussion: minimum lot size increased from 50 to 100 acres and setbacks from 200 to 300 feet. The future land use plan is being developed as an interactive Esri ArcGIS Story Map rather than a traditional document, currently at approximately 60 pages. Fire Station 18 grand opening scheduled for May 8, 2026 with open house May 9. The bowling fundraiser with the chamber is scheduled for April 29th. Trustee Hodges (Lisa) had been absent due to the flu and missed two prior meetings.
title: BOT 3-24-26
